# Science & Discovery

Topics covering scientific advances and explorations of 1776.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->

| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Buffon's Natural History](buffon-natural-history.md) | Georges-Louis Leclerc, Comte de Buffon, continued his monumental *Histoire naturelle* in 1776, advancing biogeography, an experimental estimate of Earth's age, and early speculation on species transformation. |
| [Captain Cook's Third Voyage](captain-cook-third-voyage.md) | James Cook's final Pacific expedition, departing Plymouth on July 12, 1776 aboard HMS Resolution and HMS Discovery to return Omai to the Pacific and search for the Northwest Passage. |
| [David Hume's Death and Philosophical Legacy](david-hume-1776.md) | The death of the Scottish Enlightenment philosopher on August 25, 1776, and the empiricist legacy — Hume's Fork, the problem of causation, and the is-ought distinction — that reshaped Western philosophy. |
